_comment: Render-parity reference for the Seed-OSS checkpoint's OWN chat_template.jinja (no macMLX override needed as of swift-jinja 2.4.0, which fixes the integer-keyed object-literal parse — huggingface/swift-jinja #62, reported by macMLX). Generated by docs/reference/capture_seed_oss_chat_template.py from the ORIGINAL checkpoint chat_template.jinja (jinja2, trim_blocks + lstrip_blocks, cross-checked == transformers apply_chat_template). SeedOssChatTemplateParityTests renders `template` through swift-jinja and asserts equality with each case's `expected`. `cases` are representative message-set diversity; `boundary_cases` sweep every thinking-budget reflection-interval tier boundary (±1) plus 0/negative/past-top, proving the checkpoint's integer-keyed dict + dictsort lookup renders natively for every budget.

cases.name: system_user

messages.content: What is the capital of France?

cases.expected:
<seed:bos>system
You are a helpful assistant.<seed:eos><seed:bos>user
What is the capital of France?<seed:eos><seed:bos>assistant

cases.name: multi_turn
cases.expected:
<seed:bos>user
Hi there.<seed:eos><seed:bos>assistant
Hello! How can I help?<seed:eos><seed:bos>user
Tell me a joke.<seed:eos><seed:bos>assistant

cases.name: thinking_budget_512
cases.expected:
<seed:bos>system
You are a math tutor.<seed:eos><seed:bos>system
You are an intelligent assistant with reflective ability. In the process of thinking and reasoning, you need to strictly follow the thinking budget, which is 512. That is, you need to complete your thinking within 512 tokens and start answering the user's questions. You will reflect on your thinking process every 128 tokens, stating how many tokens have been used and how many are left.
<seed:eos><seed:bos>user
What is 2+2?<seed:eos><seed:bos>assistant
